level_signals: Track record of owning product areas end-to-end
Expected to define vision, roadmap, and success metrics independently
Cross-functional credibility with ML engineers, designers, and health system executives

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 5+ years of product management experience shipping products that users love, with a track record of owning product areas end-to-end.
Builder mentality. You've personally driven products from discovery through launch and iteration. You care about craft and quality in what you ship.
Strong user empathy and product taste. You instinctively know the difference between a product that works and one that delights. You've built that instinct through direct, repeated exposure to users.
Healthcare or clinical workflow experience strongly preferred. You understand how clinicians work, how health systems buy, and why healthcare products are hard to get right.
Comfort with ambiguity. You can take a messy problem space and create a clear roadmap without waiting for perfect information.

Cross-functional credibility. You can go deep with ML engineers on model tradeoffs, partner with design on interaction details, and present strategy to health system executives. You earn trust through competence and follow-through.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: You have built ML/AI-powered products and understand how to design around model capabilities and limitations.
You have worked with Epic or other EHR platforms and understand what it takes to build great embedded experiences.
You actively use AI tools in your own work — prototyping, analysis, writing, coding — and have strong intuition for how AI products should behave and where they fall short.
You have a technical background (engineering, CS, or similar) and are comfortable going deep on system design, data models, and technical tradeoffs with engineering teams.

scope.surface: Core Product Experiences — clinical documentation and workflow products across ambulatory, emergency, and inpatient care settings; integrated with EHR environments
core_responsibilities: Own product strategy for a Core Product area including vision, roadmap, and success metrics
Conduct hands-on user research — shadowing clinicians in exam rooms and understanding friction across specialties and care settings
Partner with engineering, design, and ML to ship high-quality clinical workflows
Integrate Abridge workflows deeply with EHR environments including Epic
Define KPIs (adoption, retention, encounter share, quality scores) and drive data-informed iteration
Navigate ambiguity and create structure and clarity in complex problem spaces

compensation.equity_form: equity grants for full time employees

green_flags: Clear product surface and domain ownership described in detail
Company has significant deployed scale (250+ health systems, 80M+ clinical conversations) — product impact is measurable
Strong mission alignment around healthcare AI and clinician wellbeing
JD encourages direct user research including clinician shadowing — signals real product culture
red_flags: Work auth and visa sponsorship not mentioned
Location/hybrid policy not explicitly described despite listing three office cities — unclear which are required
Healthcare domain experience listed as 'strongly preferred' which may function as a de facto hard requirement given the clinical depth required

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from 5+ years requirement, scope of ownership, and cross-functional expectations)
scope.business_model (inferred from enterprise health system deployments)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; partially quoted from JD but synthesized)
location.type (inferred as hybrid based on office mentions and no explicit remote policy stated)
location.remote_us_eligible (inferred as false given office-centric language; not explicitly stated)
ambiguities: Healthcare/clinical workflow experience is described as 'strongly preferred' but given the depth of clinical knowledge expected in the role (clinician shadowing, EHR integrations, understanding of care settings), it may function as a hard requirement in practice. Classified as hard severity here.

Location policy is ambiguous: three offices are listed (SF, NYC, Pittsburgh) but no explicit hybrid/remote/onsite policy is stated for this role. Treated as hybrid given office presence and startup culture signals.
Visa sponsorship and work authorization are not mentioned anywhere in the JD.
